# Project Office ÔÇö how to work in this repo

This repository is linked to a **project office** ÔÇö the Task Manager backend project
`<project_name>` (`projectId: <projectId>`) ÔÇö where its tasks and comments live, kept separate
from this code repo. All project data is reached through the `project-office` CLI.

<!-- project-office:managed:start -->
## Office rules (managed ÔÇö regenerated by setup; edit "Working flow" below instead)

- **Everything project-side goes through the `project-office` CLI**, run from inside this repo:
  `project-office task:list / task:view --task TASK-1 / project:view`. The CLI resolves the
  project from `.project-office/repo-settings.json` ÔÇö no `--project` needed. Never reach the
  backend by direct API/database/filesystem, and **never hand-edit `repo-settings.json`** (only
  `project:link-repo` writes it).
- **Look up a command's exact usage** with `project-office instructions <command>` (e.g.
  `project-office instructions task:create`) rather than guessing flags.
- **Statuses:** `open Ôćĺ ready_for_development Ôćĺ in_progress Ôćĺ ready_to_test Ôćĺ completed Ôćĺ
  closed`. The agent may claim a task (`Ôćĺ in_progress`) and, once the work is implemented
  **and verified**, hand it off to testing (`Ôćĺ ready_to_test`). `ready_for_development`,
  `completed`, and `closed` are **user-side** decisions ÔÇö do not set them unless the user
  explicitly says so. There is no `Blocked` status.
- **Comments are the work log.** Progress, decisions, open questions, verification results, and
  artifacts worth keeping go in **comments** (`task:comment-add`) ÔÇö `task:update` is for the
  task's name/status/description/tags themselves.
<!-- project-office:managed:end -->

## Working flow (defaults ÔÇö edit or extend for this project)

### Tasks

- **Pick up** ÔÇö `Ôćĺ in_progress` when you actually start work, not before.
- **While working** ÔÇö log events as comments (below); keep `description` as the statement of intent, never running progress.
- **Hand off** ÔÇö `in_progress Ôćĺ ready_to_test` once implemented **and verified**, with a hand-off comment. `completed` / `closed` come later, from the user.
- **Can't proceed** ÔÇö don't park it in a status: leave it `in_progress`, comment the blocker, raise it with the user.

### Comments ÔÇö the work log

Add a comment (`task:comment-add --task TASK-1 --content "ÔÇŽ"`) on each event:

- **Picking up** ÔÇö the intent/plan in a few lines, before changing anything.
- **A non-obvious decision** ÔÇö what you chose, why, what you rejected (code shows *what*; the comment preserves *why*).
- **An open question** ÔÇö leave it visible instead of guessing.
- **Verifying** ÔÇö what exactly was checked and how; a fact, not "it works".
- **Handing off** ÔÇö the final summary (see below).

Keep the task's own state out of comments ÔÇö name / status / description / tags change through `task:update`.

Findings along the way:

- **Contextual** ("while doing X, noticed Y nearby is fragile") ÔÇö a comment on this task.
- **Standalone, needs its own action** ÔÇö create a new task (skeleton), don't bury it in an unrelated task's comments where it won't be found.

**Hand-off comment** (`Ôćĺ ready_to_test`) ÔÇö the one structured comment, two points: **what changed** and **how it was verified**. Nothing else; it exists for whoever takes the task to test.

### Documentation

Project Office has a Documentation Hub (`doc:view` / `doc:create` / `doc:update`) ÔÇö a **context
source, not a step of every task**. Rule: **read when referenced, write only when asked.**

- **Read** ÔÇö only docs the user or the task explicitly names (a `DOC-ÔÇŽ` key or a documentation
  link); read those and nothing else. Never browse or auto-read project docs.
- **Write** ÔÇö create or update a doc only on an explicit request, never as opportunistic
  cleanup. If the target doc or the change is unclear, ask.
- **Where a doc belongs** ÔÇö put it in the **office** when it is project-level or shared across
  repos (domain logic, API documentation, contracts, requirements); keep **repo-specific
  specification** in the repo itself. When unsure, ask.

### Project-specific conventions

**Multi-repo project** ÔÇö this office spans more than one repo. Run `project-office project:view`
for the current linked repos/paths (the source of truth ÔÇö don't hardcode from memory):

- `project-office-cli` (this repo) ÔÇö `/var/www/task-manager/project-office-cli`
- `mvp-task-manager` ÔÇö `/var/www/task-manager/mvp-task-manager`

**`repo:<name>` tags route a task to its repo.** Before implementing, check the task's tags:

- names a **different** repo than the one you're in Ôćĺ stop, tell the user, switch to that repo first;
- names **this** repo, or no `repo:*` tag Ôćĺ the task belongs here.
